message.content:
Adversarially verify this finding about a design doc (the doc is at /tmp/claude-1000/-home-jleechan-projects-worktree-runner/8ca589dd-8422-44b1-a543-52bd7b71f14b/scratchpad/gistfile1.txt; the repo is /home/jleechan/projects/worktree_runner; you may use WebSearch/WebFetch and read files). Try to REFUTE it. Finding from the "facts" reviewer, severity major:
TITLE: Design's assumed gha-outrunner interface (outrunner.yml, start/stop/status, count/backend/pids_limit keys) does not match the real tool
DETAIL: VERIFIED against the actual docs (https://raw.githubusercontent.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner/main/docs/reference/configuration.md and .../cli.md): (1) The config file is `config.yml` (default `/etc/outrunner/config.yml`), NOT `outrunner.yml` as src/config.rs writes. (2) There is no `backend:` key — each runner in the `runners:` map specifies exactly one of `docker:`, `libvirt:`, or `tart:` sub-blocks. (3) There is no `count:` key — concurrency is `max_runners` (default 2). (4) `memory` is an integer in MiB (default 8192), not '4G'/'4g' strings; there is no `resources:` block and no `pids_limit` option at all. (5) The CLI is a single foreground command with only --config/--url/--token/--max-runners flags — there are NO start/stop/status subcommands, so src/runner.rs's lifecycle model ('start/stop/status via gha-outrunner') is wrong; gha-isolated would have to manage the process itself (spawn/signal/pidfile or systemd unit). (6) Auth is PAT-only (token_file, --token, GITHUB_TOKEN, systemd-creds); no GitHub App auth is documented. (7) gha-outrunner has no built-in service installer, so the design's service.rs must write systemd/launchd units itself — consistent with the design but worth stating.
RECOMMENDATION: Rewrite src/config.rs to emit the real schema (config.yml with url, token_file, runners.<name>.{labels, docker|libvirt|tart, max_runners}, integer MiB memory) and redesign runner.rs as a process supervisor (spawn foreground outrunner, SIGINT for clean shutdown) rather than assuming subcommands. Drop pids_limit from the config or file an upstream feature request.
Is the finding factually correct and does the recommendation make sense? If the factual basis is wrong or the severity is inflated, refute it. Default to refuted=true only if you have concrete contrary evidence or the claim is unsupported after checking.

input.description: Inspect design doc for outrunner references

input.prompt: Describe the full configuration schema: config file name and default path, top-level keys, per-runner keys, whether there is a 'backend' key, a 'count' key, a 'max_runners' key, memory format (integer MiB vs strings like '4G'), whether there is a 'resources' block or 'pids_limit' option, and how auth is configured (token_file, GitHub App, etc.). Quote relevant sections.

Describe the CLI interface: is it a single foreground command or does it have subcommands like start/stop/status/install? List all flags and subcommands documented. Also note any service installation features.

content.content:
# outrunner Configuration Schema Summary

**File & Location:**
Default config file is `config.yml` at `/etc/outrunner/config.yml`, with CLI override via `--config` flag.

**Top-Level Keys:**
- `url`: "Global repository or org URL"
- `token_file`: "Global path to a file containing the GitHub token"
- `runners`: Map of scale set configurations

**Per-Runner Keys:**
Each runner in the `runners` map requires:
- `labels`: "An array of labels registered on this runner's scale set"
- Exactly one backend: `docker`, `libvirt`, or `tart`
- Optional: `url`, `token_file`, `max_runners`

**Backend Selection:**
There is no dedicated 'backend' key. Instead, runners specify one of three backend blocks (`docker`, `libvirt`, or `tart`), each containing backend-specific configuration.

**No 'count' Key:**
Configuration uses `max_runners` instead: "Maximum number of concurrent runners for this scale set."

**Memory Format:**
Memory is specified as integers in MiB. Example: `memory: 8192` (not string format like '4G').

**No 'resources' or 'pids_limit':**
These options don't appear in the schema.

**Authentication:**
Token resolution follows a precedence chain: per-runner `token_file`, `--token` CLI flag, `GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variable, systemd-creds path, then global `token_file`. No GitHub App authentication is documented.

# CLI Interface Analysis

## Command Structure

outrunner is a **single foreground command** with no subcommands. It runs continuously as a listener process rather than offering discrete operations like start/stop/status.

According to the documentation, the tool "registers a GitHub Actions scale set on a repository or organization, then listens for jobs and provisions ephemeral runner environments for each one."

## Flags

The CLI supports these options:

| Flag | Type | Default |
|------|------|---------|
| `--config` | string | `/etc/outrunner/config.yml` |
| `--url` | string | (none) |
| `--token` | string | (none) |
| `--max-runners` | int | `2` |
| `-h, --help` | | Help text |
| `-v, --version` | | Version info |

## No Subcommands

The interface includes no documented subcommands. All functionality flows through the single `outrunner` invocation with optional flags.

## Service Integration

Rather than built-in installation features, the documentation suggests deploying outrunner as a systemd service. The tool integrates with systemd-creds for secure credential storage: "For production deployments, use systemd-creds (encrypted at rest) or an environment file."

Installation methods cover package managers (Homebrew, apt, dnf) and source compilation, but service setup itself appears to be external configuration.

Web search results for query: "gha-outrunner GitHub Actions runner tool"

Links: [{"title":"GitHub - NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner: Ephemeral VM and container runners for GitHub Actions — no Kubernetes required","url":"https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner"},{"title":"GitHub - omsf/gha-runner: A simple GitHub Action for creating cloud-based self-hosted runners. · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/omsf/gha-runner"},{"title":"GitHub - rust-lang/gha-runner: Custom fork of the GitHub Actions runner","url":"https://github.com/rust-lang/gha-runner"},{"title":"GitHub - Leapfrog-DevOps/gha: Powerful CLI tool for local GitHub Actions workflow management · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/Leapfrog-DevOps/gha"},{"title":"Get started with Actions Runner Controller - GitHub Docs","url":"https://docs.github.com/en/actions/tutorials/use-actions-runner-controller/get-started"},{"title":"GitHub - PasseiDireto/gh-runner: Self Hosted GitHub Actions Runner · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/PasseiDireto/gh-runner"},{"title":"gha-runner","url":"https://gha-runner.readthedocs.io/en/latest/"},{"title":"GitHub - actions/runner: The Runner for GitHub Actions :rocket: · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/actions/runner"},{"title":"gha-runner · Actions · GitHub Marketplace · GitHub","url":"https://github.com/marketplace/actions/gha-runner"}]

Based on the web search results, here's information about the gha-outrunner GitHub Actions runner tool:

## Overview

gha-outrunner provides ephemeral GitHub Actions runners without requiring Kubernetes, provisioning fresh Docker containers or VMs for each GitHub Actions job and then destroying them when the job completes.

## Key Features

It uses GitHub's scaleset API to register as an autoscaling runner group. outrunner gives you the same isolation guarantees with Docker, libvirt, or Tart.

## Advantages Over Alternatives

GitHub's Actions Runner Controller (ARC) requires Kubernetes, but if you're running on bare metal or a simple VPS, you shouldn't need a cluster just to get ephemeral runners. No additional orchestrator is needed.

## Supported Backends

The tool supports multiple provisioning options including Docker containers for fastest startup, as well as KVM VMs and Tart VMs for more isolated environments.

The official repository is available at https://github.com/NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner.


REMINDER: You MUST include the sources above in your response to the user using markdown hyperlinks.

input.reasoning: Attempted refutation failed on every point. I independently fetched the upstream docs (configuration.md and cli.md from NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner) and read the design doc. All seven claims verified: (1) real config is config.yml at /etc/outrunner/config.yml, not outrunner.yml as design's src/config.rs writes (line 248); (2) no backend: key — runners take exactly one docker:/libvirt:/tart: sub-block; (3) no count: key — max_runners/--max-runners (default 2) governs concurrency; (4) memory is integer MiB, no resources: block, no pids_limit anywhere (design emits 'memory: 4G', 'resources:', and 'pids_limit: 100' at lines 223-242); (5) CLI is a single foreground command with only --config/--url/--token/--max-runners — no start/stop/status subcommands, contradicting design lines 25/74 ('start/stop/status via gha-outrunner'); (6) auth is PAT-only (token_file/--token/GITHUB_TOKEN/systemd-creds), no GitHub App auth; (7) no built-in service installer. Web search confirms NetwindHQ/gha-outrunner is the only tool with this name, so the design cannot be referring to some other tool with the assumed interface. Severity 'major' is appropriate: the design's entire integration surface (config schema and lifecycle model) does not match the real tool and would fail at first run. The recommendation (emit the real schema, redesign runner.rs as a process supervisor with signal-based shutdown, drop pids_limit or file upstream) is technically sound. Only trivial imprecision found: the finding calls 8192 MiB the 'default' memory where docs present it as the default/example value — immaterial to the conclusion.
